title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_en: Repel the evil deed with one which is better, then lo! He, between whom and you there was enmity (will become) as though he was a bosom friend." Some people have no solution to their problems and nor is there a way to reform them. You can only deal with them in accordance with their own wishes. In that case, you can either have patience with them, or leave them. It is mentioned that a man once travelled with a businessman. This man would do all the work while travelling, such as serving his companion, ofﬂoading the luggage and feeding the camels, until he would become exhausted. On the way back, the two of them stopped to have their meal. They made their camels kneel and dismounted. The man laid down outstretched on the ground while his businessman companion laid out a sheet, ofﬂoaded the luggage, and then turned to him and said, "Why don't you gather the wood while I cut the meat?" He replied, "By Allah, I am very tired after riding the camel all this time!" The businessman then stood and gathered the wood, and said, "Can you make ﬁre?" He replied, "The smoke stings me if I come near it", so the man lit the ﬁre himself. The busineesman then said, "Can you please help me in cutting the meat?" He replied, "I fear that I may cut my hand", hence, the man cut the meat himself. The businessman then said, "Could you put the meat in the pan and cook the meal?" He replied, "It is very tiring for me to watch the meal before it is properly cooked". The businessman then began to cook himself. Respond to mistreatment with kindness When he ﬁnally ﬁnished cooking and felt very tired, he lay down on the ground, and said, "Could you please lay out the dining mat, and pour the meal out onto the plates?" He replied, "I am feeling really heavy, and I don't have the energy to do that right now." The businessman then got up, prepared the meal and laid it out on the mat. The businessman said, "Would you come over and eat?" He replied, "By Allah, I feel very embarrassed at constantly making excuses, so I shall relent this time!" He then got up and ate the meal! You might meet people who are like him. Do not be saddened by this. Rather, be ﬁrm like a mountain. Our model educator, the Prophet ,would deal with people rationally and not emotionally. He would bear other people's mistakes and still be gentle with them. Just imagine! There he was, sitting in a blessed gathering, surrounded by his companions, and in came a Bedouin asking him for his help with paying off blood-money. This man − or an acquaintance of his − had killed someone, and so he wanted the Prophet to help him ﬁnancially to enable him to pay the blood-money to the victim's family. The Prophet then gave him something and enquired out of kindness, "Have I been good to you?" The Bedouin said, "No! You have neither been good nor courteous!" Upon hearing this, some Muslims became angry and were about to ﬁght him, but the Prophet signalled to them not to. Respond to mistreatment with kindness The Prophet then walked towards his house, called the Bedouin and said, "You came to us, asked for our help, and we helped you. But then you said to us what you said." Then the Prophet gave him more of what he could ﬁnd in his house and said, "Have I been good to you?" The Bedouin said, "Yes! May Allah reward you in your family and your kinsfolk." The Prophet was delighted to know that the Bedouin was pleased, but he was afraid that his Companions might still have hatred towards him, or that one of them may see him in the street and show his resentment towards the Bedouin. Thus, he wanted to remove this feeling from their hearts. He said to the Bedouin, "You came to us for help and we gave it to you, but then you said what you said. My Companions are resentful towards you for this reason. If you were to go to them now and say to them what you have said to me now, perhaps this feeling would be removed from their hearts." When the Bedouin came, the Prophet said, "This friend of ours came to us for help and we gave it to him, but then he said what he said. We then called him over and gave him more, and now he says that he is pleased." He then turned to the Bedouin and said, "Isn't that so?" The Bedouin replied, "Yes! May Allah reward you in your family and your kin." When the Bedouin decided to leave and go to his family, the Prophet decided to give his Companions a lesson in how to win people's hearts.
content_id: Tolaklah kejahatan dengan kebaikan, maka orang yang antara kamu dan dia ada permusuhan seolah-olah akan menjadi teman yang akrab." Ada orang-orang yang tidak ada jalan keluar bagi masalah mereka dan tidak ada cara untuk memperbaiki mereka. Kamu hanya bisa memperlakukan mereka sesuai keinginan mereka. Dalam hal ini, kamu bisa bersabar menghadapi mereka atau meninggalkan mereka. Dikisahkan bahwa seorang lelaki pernah bepergian bersama seorang pedagang. Lelaki ini mengerjakan semua pekerjaan selama perjalanan — melayani temannya, menurunkan muatan, dan memberi makan unta — hingga dia kelelahan. Dalam perjalanan pulang, keduanya berhenti untuk makan. Mereka menambatkan unta dan turun. Lelaki itu berbaring di tanah sementara temannya si pedagang menggelar alas, menurunkan muatan, lalu berbalik kepadanya dan berkata, "Mengapa kamu tidak mengumpulkan kayu bakar sementara aku memotong daging?" Dia menjawab, "Demi Allah, aku sangat lelah setelah menunggang unta sepanjang waktu ini!" Si pedagang pun berdiri dan mengumpulkan kayu bakar sendiri, lalu berkata, "Bisakah kamu menyalakan api?" Dia menjawab, "Asapnya menusuk mataku jika aku mendekat." Maka lelaki itu menyalakan api sendiri. Si pedagang kemudian berkata, "Bisakah kamu membantuku memotong daging?" Dia menjawab, "Aku khawatir akan memotong tanganku sendiri." Maka lelaki itu memotong daging sendiri. Si pedagang berkata, "Bisakah kamu meletakkan daging di wajan dan memasak makanan?" Dia menjawab, "Sungguh sangat melelahkan mengawasi masakan sebelum matang." Maka si pedagang pun memasak sendiri. Ketika akhirnya selesai memasak dan merasa sangat lelah, dia berbaring di tanah dan berkata, "Bisakah kamu menggelar alas makan dan menuangkan makanan ke piring?" Dia menjawab, "Aku terasa sangat berat dan tidak punya tenaga untuk itu sekarang." Si pedagang pun bangkit, menyiapkan makanan, dan meletakkannya di atas alas. Si pedagang berkata, "Maukah kamu datang dan makan?" Dia menjawab, "Demi Allah, aku merasa sangat malu karena terus-menerus membuat alasan, jadi kali ini aku akan menurut!" Lalu dia bangkit dan makan! Kamu mungkin bertemu orang-orang seperti dia. Janganlah bersedih. Sebaliknya, tegarlah seperti gunung. Teladan kita, Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bergaul dengan manusia secara rasional, bukan emosional. Beliau menanggung kesalahan orang lain namun tetap lemah lembut terhadap mereka. Bayangkan! Beliau sedang duduk dalam sebuah majelis yang penuh berkah, dikelilingi para sahabat, ketika tiba-tiba seorang Badui datang meminta bantuan untuk membayar diyat. Lelaki ini — atau kenalannya — telah membunuh seseorang, dan dia ingin Rasulullah membantunya secara finansial agar bisa membayar diyat kepada keluarga korban. Rasulullah memberinya sesuatu dan bertanya dengan penuh kebaikan, "Sudahkah aku berbuat baik kepadamu?" Si Badui berkata, "Tidak! Kamu tidak berbuat baik dan tidak pula bersikap sopan!" Mendengar ini, beberapa Muslim marah dan hampir menyerangnya, tetapi Rasulullah memberi isyarat agar mereka tidak melakukannya. Rasulullah kemudian berjalan menuju rumahnya, memanggil si Badui, dan berkata, "Kamu datang kepada kami, meminta bantuan, dan kami membantumu. Namun kemudian kamu berkata seperti itu kepada kami." Rasulullah lalu memberinya lagi dari apa yang ada di rumahnya dan bertanya, "Sudahkah aku berbuat baik kepadamu?" Si Badui berkata, "Ya! Semoga Allah membalasmu kebaikan pada keluarga dan kerabatmu." Rasulullah senang mengetahui si Badui sudah puas, namun beliau khawatir para sahabatnya masih menyimpan kebencian kepadanya, atau salah satu dari mereka mungkin melihatnya di jalan dan menunjukkan rasa benci. Maka beliau ingin menghapus perasaan itu dari hati mereka. Beliau berkata kepada si Badui, "Kamu datang kepada kami untuk meminta bantuan dan kami memberikannya, tetapi kemudian kamu berkata seperti itu. Para sahabatku masih menyimpan rasa benci kepadamu karena itu. Jika kamu pergi kepada mereka sekarang dan mengatakan kepada mereka apa yang kamu katakan kepadaku, mudah-mudahan perasaan itu akan hilang dari hati mereka." Ketika si Badui datang, Rasulullah berkata, "Teman kita ini datang kepada kami meminta bantuan dan kami memberikannya, lalu dia berkata seperti itu. Kami kemudian memanggilnya dan memberinya lebih banyak, dan sekarang dia menyatakan bahwa dia puas." Beliau kemudian berpaling kepada si Badui dan bertanya, "Bukankah demikian?" Si Badui menjawab, "Ya! Semoga Allah membalasmu kebaikan pada keluarga dan kerabatmu." Ketika si Badui hendak pergi, Rasulullah memutuskan untuk memberi pelajaran kepada para sahabatnya tentang cara memenangkan hati manusia.
